notes: King of Macedon who created one of the largest empires of the ancient world through his military conquests.

docs.title: BOOK XVI
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: For Alexander, having placed a brazen shell on the ground beneath him, used to hold a silver ball in his hand, which he kept stretched outside his bed, so that when sleep pervading his whole body had relaxed the rigour of his muscles, the rattling of the ball falling might banish

docs.title: BOOK XXV
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: He was of an unsteady disposition; but this fault he corrected by an excellent plan, allowing people to set him right when guilty of indiscretion. 17. He was a frequent talker, rarely silent.

docs.title: Life of Severus Alexander
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
